steps.description: Click the "App features" link in the navigation menu on the left side of the "Apps: Developer Portal" dashboard interface.
Click the "Bot" feature card under the "App features" section in the Microsoft Teams Developer Portal interface.
Click on the "Enter a bot ID" input field in the "Identify your bot" section of the "Bots" page in the Microsoft Teams Developer Portal interface.
Click on the bot ID input field labeled "Enter a bot ID" within the "Identify your bot" section of the Microsoft Teams Developer Portal interface.
Click on the "Enter a bot ID" input field within the "Identify your bot" section of the Developer Portal interface.
Type text:${var:botid:b34bebd8-88a8-4474-9b16-104999a0a869} into the "Enter a bot ID" input field in the "Identify your bot" section of the Microsoft Teams Developer Portal interface.
Click the "Save" button at the bottom-right corner of the "Identify your bot" configuration section in the Microsoft Teams Developer Portal interface.
Click on the "App features" navigation item in the left-hand sidebar within the Microsoft Teams Developer Portal interface.
Press the "End" key to scroll to the bottom of the "App features" configuration page in the Microsoft Teams Developer Portal.
@assertion 'Create new bot(s) for debugging' is exist
Click the checkbox labeled "Deselect to keep the original botId value" in the dropdown menu of Visual Studio Code.
Click the "TypeScript" option in the "Programming Language" dropdown menu.
Click on the "Default folder" text field within the "Workspace Folder" dialog to select or modify the path `/home/vscode/AgentsToolkitProjects`.
Press the 'Enter' key to confirm the application name entered in the "Input an application name" text box within the Visual Studio Code interface.
